title: cystopteris affinis
intro: Cystopteris affinis, also known as the autumn spleenwort or autumn fern, is a species of fern native to Europe, Asia, and North America. It is a delicate, deciduous fern with intricately divided leaves, often found growing in woodland habitats and shaded areas. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems. The leaves and roots of the Cystopteris affinis plant can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote health and well-being.
